title: Project Manager - Main Component Exchanges (m/f/d)
required_skills: Bachelor's degree in STEM, commerce, or project management
Proven and demonstrable experience planning and executing complex offshore wind projects in Northern and Central Europe with multiple interfaces and subcontractors
Sound practical knowledge of UK regulatory and safety frameworks: CDM 2015 and Wind Turbine Safety Rules (WTSR)
Understanding of quality systems including structured project documentation management
Understanding of vessel mobilisation, POB planning, and offshore logistics
Strong planning and coordination skills; tool proficiency in MS Office, ideally MS Project and SharePoint
Fluency in English (German is an advantage)
Strong HSEQ mindset and ability to drive safe execution in offshore environments
matched_skills: Master degree (Leuphana, Master of Management & Entrepreneurship) — satisfies degree requirement
Fluent English (C1) — satisfies English fluency requirement
MS Office proficiency — satisfies MS Office requirement
Project planning and coordination: sprint planning at Hapag-Lloyd, 6-workstream programme coordination at HSBC — limited generic match for planning/coordination skills
German B1 — partially satisfies German advantage bonus
missing_skills: No offshore wind project management experience — this is the core requirement and is explicitly required as 'proven and demonstrable'
No knowledge of CDM 2015 or Wind Turbine Safety Rules (WTSR) — regulatory/safety frameworks required
No vessel mobilisation, POB planning, or offshore logistics experience
No HSEQ mindset or offshore safety execution experience
No MS Project or SharePoint experience (ideally required)
No subcontractor management in engineering/construction context
No STEM background or technical engineering knowledge
core_responsibilities: Lead MCE projects from handover and planning through execution and close-out
Build and maintain integrated project plans (scope, milestones, resources, logistics, marine/weather windows)
Coordinate interfaces across internal teams, customers and subcontractors; ensure clear governance and communication
Present projects to steering committee with structure, coherence, and transparency
Manage project risk, change and decision-making to secure predictable delivery
Ensure robust project documentation, reporting, cost tracking and lessons learned
Support and adhere to HSEQ standards in all activities
recommended_emphasis: This role is not suitable — do not apply
Offshore wind project management requires domain-specific technical knowledge (CDM, WTSR, vessel ops, HSEQ) that cannot be bridged from PMO/coordination experience
The JD explicitly requires 'proven and demonstrable' offshore wind experience in Northern/Central Europe — this is a hard gate
summary: Poor match at 27.3%. This is a technical offshore wind project management role requiring proven experience delivering main component exchange projects offshore, with specific regulatory knowledge (CDM 2015, WTSR), vessel logistics, and HSEQ expertise. The candidate's PMO and agile coordination background has no meaningful overlap with offshore engineering project execution. Not recommended for application.
